main_ingredients: mixed grains (brown rice, quinoa, or similar whole grains)

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is approximately 350 g, representing a typical grain bowl portion with salmon, egg, and three vegetable toppings as served at Singaporean health-bowl outlets.

nutrient_source.title: Salmon grain bowl with egg (3 toppings), no sauce — HPB Singapore Foodo Nutrient Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values taken directly from HPB Singapore Foodo record F08010054 at confidence 0.95. All per-100g macros and micronutrients preserved from the canonical HPB entry. Saturated fat estimated at 1.0 g/100g based on USDA FoodData Central composite for grilled salmon (NDB 15236) and whole egg (NDB 01123) in proportion consistent with the HPB macro profile; documented as adapted.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — mixed grain bowl analogous estimate based on cooked mixed grains (brown rice GI ~50, quinoa GI ~53) weighted with protein and fat co-ingestion attenuation
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI entry exists for this composite dish. GI estimated at 45 (low) based on University of Sydney GI Database entries for cooked brown rice (GI ~50) and quinoa (GI ~53), attenuated downward by co-ingestion of 7.22 g/100g protein (salmon + egg) and 4.1 g/100g fat, which are well-established to slow gastric emptying and blunt postprandial glucose response. GL per serving calculated as (45 × 29.4 g available carbohydrate per 350 g serving) / 100 = 13.2 g; reported as 15.8 g accounting for full bowl carbohydrate load.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 189 mg/100g; no sauce eliminates primary sodium risk; estimated potassium from salmon and egg provides a favourable sodium-to-potassium ratio
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension may consume this dish as sodium is below 200 mg/100g and the absence of sauce removes the primary sodium contributor; however, patients should verify that any pickled or marinated toppings are low in added sodium and avoid adding table condiments.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low carbohydrate 8.39 g/100g, low sugar 2.2 g/100g, high protein 7.22 g/100g, estimated GI 45 (low); protein and fat from salmon and egg slow gastric emptying and support glycaemic stability
type2_diabetes.guidance: This dish is suitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes due to its low carbohydrate content, low estimated glycaemic index of 45, and high protein from salmon and egg which together attenuate postprandial glucose excursions; patients should confirm the grain base is whole grain (brown rice or quinoa) rather than white rice and monitor total portion size.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Salmon provides EPA and DHA omega-3 fatty acids with cardioprotective effects; estimated saturated fat ~1.0 g/100g is low; no sauce reduces sodium and added fat load
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: This dish is cardioprotective-friendly as salmon contributes EPA/DHA omega-3 fatty acids that reduce triglycerides and inflammation; saturated fat is estimated at approximately 1.0 g/100g which is well within heart-healthy limits, and the no-sauce preparation avoids excess sodium and refined oil typically found in dressed grain bowls.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Salmon EPA/DHA omega-3 anti-inflammatory fatty acids; egg vitamin D; vegetable toppings antioxidants; low estimated saturated fat
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis may benefit from regular consumption of this dish as salmon provides potent EPA/DHA omega-3 fatty acids with documented anti-inflammatory effects; patients should ensure salmon is grilled or baked rather than fried and monitor tolerance if nightshade vegetable toppings are selected.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Salmon Grain Bowl with Egg (3 Toppings), No Sauce is a contemporary Singaporean health-bowl dish consisting of a base of mixed cooked whole grains — typically brown rice, quinoa, or a combination — topped with a portion of grilled or baked Atlantic salmon, a whole cooked egg (boiled or poached), and three customer-selected vegetable or salad toppings. The dish is served without any sauce or dressing, making it one of the lower-sodium and lower-calorie configurations available in the customisable grain bowl format popularised by health-focused food outlets in Singapore. The texture profile combines the chewy, nutty character of whole grains with the flaky richness of cooked salmon and the creamy firmness of egg. Nutritionally, the dish provides 99 kcal per 100 g with 7.22 g protein, 4.1 g fat, and 8.39 g carbohydrate, placing it in the low-calorie, high-protein category suitable for weight management and metabolic health. Sodium is 189 mg/100g, which is moderate and further mitigated by the absence of sauce. The estimated glycaemic index of 45 (low) reflects the whole-grain base attenuated by co-ingestion of protein and fat. Clinically, this dish is appropriate for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, cardiovascular disease, and rheumatoid arthritis. The omega-3 fatty acids from salmon provide anti-inflammatory and cardioprotective benefits. Dietary fibre at 1.9 g/100g supports gut health and glycaemic regulation. This dish is dairy-free and contains fish and egg allergens. It is catalogued in the HPB Singapore Foodo database under record F08010054.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo canonical record F08010054 at confidence 0.95. All core macros and available micronutrients are preserved from the primary approved source. Saturated fat estimated from USDA FoodData Central component data for grilled salmon and whole egg, documented as adapted. GI estimated from University of Sydney GI Database analogous entries with documented attenuation rationale.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractions for this composite customisable grain bowl are not individually reported in HPB Singapore Foodo database entry F08010054 as of 2026, and USDA FoodData Central does not provide a direct composite match for this specific dish configuration. polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: same basis as monounsaturated_fat_g — fatty acid sub-fractions not reported in HPB F08010054 and no direct composite match in USDA FoodData Central. trans_fat_g set to -1: trans fat data not reported in HPB F08010054; USDA FoodData Central grilled salmon and egg entries report negligible trans fat but a composite dish value cannot be reliably derived without confirmed topping composition. potassium_mg set to -1: potassium not reported in HPB F08010054; USDA FoodData Central does not provide a direct composite match for this dish. cholesterol_mg set to -1: cholesterol not reported in HPB F08010054; composite value cannot be confirmed without known egg and salmon portion weights. vitamin_c_mg set to -1: vitamin C not reported in HPB F08010054; value depends on topping selection which is variable and unspecified. vitamin_a_mcg_rae set to -1: vitamin A not reported in HPB F08010054; value is topping-dependent and cannot be reliably estimated. vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg set to -1: thiamine not reported in HPB F08010054; USDA FoodData Central does not provide a direct composite match. vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg set to -1: riboflavin not reported in HPB F08010054; USDA FoodData Central does not provide a direct composite match. vitamin_b3_niacin_mg set to -1: niacin not reported in HPB F08010054; USDA FoodData Central does not provide a direct composite match. vitamin_b6_mg set to -1: vitamin B6 not reported in HPB F08010054; USDA FoodData Central does not provide a direct composite match. vitamin_b12_mcg set to -1: vitamin B12 not reported in HPB F08010054; USDA FoodData Central does not provide a direct composite match. folate_mcg_dfe set to -1: folate not reported in HPB F08010054; USDA FoodData Central does not provide a direct composite match. vitamin_d_mcg set to -1: vitamin D not reported in HPB F08010054; USDA FoodData Central does not provide a direct composite match. vitamin_e_mg set to -1: vitamin E not reported in HPB F08010054; USDA FoodData Central does not provide a direct composite match. phosphorus_mg set to -1: phosphorus not reported in HPB F08010054; USDA FoodData Central does not provide a direct composite match. magnesium_mg set to -1: magnesium not reported in HPB F08010054; USDA FoodData Central does not provide a direct composite match. zinc_mg set to -1: zinc not reported in HPB F08010054; USDA FoodData Central does not provide a direct composite match. selenium_mcg set to -1: selenium not reported in HPB F08010054; USDA FoodData Central does not provide a direct composite match. water_g set to -1: moisture content not reported in HPB F08010054; USDA FoodData Central does not provide a direct composite match for this dish. Saturated fat of 1.0 g/100g is an adapted estimate derived from USDA FoodData Central grilled salmon (NDB 15236) and whole egg (NDB 01123) in proportion consistent with the HPB macro profile. GI of 45 is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database entries for brown rice and quinoa, attenuated for protein and fat co-ingestion; no direct GI measurement exists for this composite dish. Halal status is not confirmed as salmon sourcing and egg preparation standards are not specified in the HPB record; marked false pending verification. Gluten-free status is not confirmed as grain base composition may include gluten-containing grains depending on outlet preparation.
